name: Cleopatra VII
description: Last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom of Egypt

name: Julius Caesar
description: Roman general and statesman who played a critical role in the fall of the Roman Republic

name: Alexander the Great
description: Created one of the largest empires in ancient history

name: Nefertiti
description: Great Royal Wife of Pharaoh Akhenaten

name: Confucius
description: Chinese philosopher whose teachings deeply influenced East Asian life and thought

name: Socrates
description: Classical Greek philosopher credited as founder of Western philosophy

name: Genghis Khan
description: Founder and first Great Khan of the Mongol Empire

name: Leonardo da Vinci
description: Renaissance polymath whose areas of interest included painting, sculpting, and science

name: Queen Elizabeth I
description: Queen of England and Ireland, the last of the five monarchs of the House of Tudor

name: William Shakespeare
description: English playwright and poet, widely regarded as the greatest writer in the English language

name: Isaac Newton
description: Mathematician, physicist, and astronomer who formulated the laws of motion and gravity

name: Marie Antoinette
description: Last Queen of France before the French Revolution

name: Napoleon Bonaparte
description: French military leader who rose to prominence during the French Revolution

name: Abraham Lincoln
description: 16th President of the United States who led the nation through the Civil War

name: Queen Victoria
description: Queen of the United Kingdom whose reign marked the Victorian era

name: Harriet Tubman
description: American abolitionist and political activist who escaped slavery and led rescue missions

name: Florence Nightingale
description: Founder of modern nursing and statistician

name: Sitting Bull
description: Hunkpapa Lakota leader who led his people during years of resistance to US government policies

name: Nikola Tesla
description: Inventor, electrical engineer, and futurist known for contributions to AC electricity

name: Mahatma Gandhi
description: Leader of the Indian independence movement through nonviolent civil disobedience

name: Marie Curie
description: Physicist and chemist who conducted pioneering research on radioactivity

name: Winston Churchill
description: Prime Minister of the United Kingdom during World War II

name: Albert Einstein
description: Theoretical physicist who developed the theory of relativity

name: Frida Kahlo
description: Mexican painter known for her portraits, self-portraits, and works inspired by nature

name: Martin Luther King Jr.
description: American Baptist minister and activist who was the most visible leader of the civil rights movement

name: Ramesses II
description: Third pharaoh of the Nineteenth Dynasty, often regarded as Egypt's most powerful pharaoh

name: Hatshepsut
description: Fifth pharaoh of the Eighteenth Dynasty, one of the few female pharaohs

name: Sun Tzu
description: Chinese general and military strategist, author of The Art of War

name: Plato
description: Athenian philosopher who founded the Academy, the first institution of higher learning

name: Aristotle
description: Greek philosopher and polymath who founded the Lyceum

name: Hannibal Barca
description: Carthaginian general who famously crossed the Alps with elephants

name: Augustus Caesar
description: First Roman Emperor and founder of the Roman Principate

name: Marcus Aurelius
description: Roman emperor and Stoic philosopher, author of Meditations

name: Attila the Hun
description: Ruler of the Huns who was one of the most feared enemies of Rome

name: Charlemagne
description: King of the Franks and first Holy Roman Emperor

name: Saladin
description: First sultan of Egypt and Syria who led the Muslim reconquest of Jerusalem

name: Marco Polo
description: Venetian merchant traveler whose travels are recorded in The Travels of Marco Polo

name: Joan of Arc
description: French heroine who led the French army to victories during the Hundred Years War

name: Michelangelo
description: Renaissance sculptor, painter, architect, and poet of the High Renaissance

name: Raphael
description: Italian painter and architect of the High Renaissance

name: Martin Luther
description: German professor and key figure in the Protestant Reformation

name: Henry VIII
description: King of England known for his six marriages and break with the Catholic Church

name: Galileo Galilei
description: Italian astronomer and physicist, father of observational astronomy

name: Rembrandt van Rijn
description: Dutch Golden Age painter regarded as one of the greatest visual artists in history

name: Louis XIV
description: King of France known as the Sun King, longest-reigning monarch in European history

name: Peter the Great
description: Tsar of Russia who modernized and expanded the Russian state

name: Benjamin Franklin
description: Founding Father, scientist, inventor, and diplomat

name: Catherine the Great
description: Empress of Russia who presided over the Russian Enlightenment

name: George Washington
description: First President of the United States and Commander-in-Chief during the Revolutionary War

name: Thomas Jefferson
description: Third President and principal author of the Declaration of Independence

name: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
description: Prolific and influential composer of the Classical period

name: Ludwig van Beethoven
description: German composer and pianist, a crucial figure in the transition to the Romantic era

name: Simon Bolivar
description: Venezuelan military and political leader who liberated much of South America

name: Charles Darwin
description: English naturalist who proposed the theory of evolution by natural selection

name: Frederick Douglass
description: American social reformer, writer, and statesman who escaped from slavery

name: Karl Marx
description: German philosopher and economist, author of Das Kapital

name: Leo Tolstoy
description: Russian writer regarded as one of the greatest authors of all time

name: Geronimo
description: Prominent leader and medicine man from the Bedonkohe band of the Apache tribe

name: Vincent van Gogh
description: Post-Impressionist painter who created about 2,100 artworks

name: Teddy Roosevelt
description: 26th President known for conservation efforts and the Panama Canal

name: Sun Yat-sen
description: Chinese revolutionary and political leader, first president of the Republic of China

name: Rosa Luxemburg
description: Marxist theorist, philosopher, and revolutionary socialist

name: Mustafa Kemal Ataturk
description: Founder and first president of the Republic of Turkey

name: Coco Chanel
description: French fashion designer who founded the Chanel brand

name: Mao Zedong
description: Chinese communist revolutionary and founding father of the People's Republic of China

name: Amelia Earhart
description: American aviation pioneer and the first female aviator to fly solo across the Atlantic

name: Ernest Hemingway
description: American novelist and Nobel Prize laureate

name: Haile Selassie
description: Emperor of Ethiopia from 1930 to 1974

name: Nelson Mandela
description: Anti-apartheid revolutionary and first president of post-apartheid South Africa

name: Eva Peron
description: First Lady of Argentina and a powerful political figure

name: Che Guevara
description: Argentine Marxist revolutionary and key figure in the Cuban Revolution

name: Anne Frank
description: Jewish girl whose diary of life in hiding during the Holocaust became world-famous

name: Tutankhamun
description: Egyptian pharaoh of the 18th Dynasty known for his nearly intact tomb

name: Boudicca
description: Queen of the Iceni tribe who led an uprising against the Roman occupation of Britain

name: Kublai Khan
description: Fifth Khagan of the Mongol Empire and founder of the Yuan dynasty

name: Suleiman the Magnificent
description: Longest-reigning Sultan of the Ottoman Empire

name: Akbar the Great
description: Third Mughal Emperor who expanded the empire to include most of the Indian subcontinent

name: Tokugawa Ieyasu
description: Founder of the Tokugawa shogunate that ruled Japan for 250 years

name: Pocahontas
description: Native American woman associated with the colonial settlement at Jamestown

name: Voltaire
description: French Enlightenment writer, historian, and philosopher

name: Toussaint Louverture
description: Leader of the Haitian Revolution, the most successful slave revolt in history

name: Shaka Zulu
description: King of the Zulu Kingdom who unified many tribes into the Zulu nation

name: Sojourner Truth
description: African American abolitionist and women's rights activist

name: Giuseppe Garibaldi
description: Italian general and nationalist who contributed to Italian unification

name: Empress Dowager Cixi
description: Chinese empress dowager and regent who effectively controlled the government for decades

name: Rasputin
description: Russian mystic and self-proclaimed holy man who gained influence in the court of Tsar Nicholas II

name: Pablo Picasso
description: Spanish painter, sculptor, and co-founder of Cubism

name: Pancho Villa
description: Mexican revolutionary general and one of the most prominent figures of the Mexican Revolution

name: Rani Lakshmibai
description: Queen of the princely state of Jhansi and key figure in the Indian Rebellion of 1857

name: Ada Lovelace
description: English mathematician recognized as the first computer programmer

name: Mary Shelley
description: English novelist who wrote Frankenstein, considered the first science fiction novel

name: Hypatia
description: Greek mathematician, astronomer, and philosopher in Alexandria, Egypt

name: Murasaki Shikibu
description: Japanese novelist, poet, and lady-in-waiting, author of The Tale of Genji

name: Mansa Musa
description: Emperor of the Mali Empire, often considered the wealthiest person in history

name: Ibn Battuta
description: Moroccan scholar and explorer who traveled over 70,000 miles across the known world

name: Montezuma II
description: Ninth ruler of the Aztec Empire, during the Spanish conquest

name: Zheng He
description: Chinese mariner, explorer, and diplomat who led massive naval expeditions

name: Pachacuti
description: Ninth Sapa Inca who transformed the Inca state into the Inca Empire

name: Sappho
description: Archaic Greek poet from the island of Lesbos, one of the first known female poets

name: Chanakya
description: Ancient Indian polymath who authored the Arthashastra, served as chief advisor to Emperor Chandragupta Maurya, and is considered the pioneer of political science and economics in India
physical_description: Elderly Brahmin scholar with a shaved head except for a sikha (tuft), sharp angular features, and an austere bearing
clothing_notes: Simple white cotton dhoti and uttariya (upper cloth), sacred thread (yajnopavita) across the chest, wooden sandals

name: Ashoka
description: Third emperor of the Maurya dynasty who ruled nearly all of the Indian subcontinent, converted to Buddhism after the Kalinga War, and spread the principles of dharma through rock and pillar edicts across his empire
physical_description: Broad-shouldered and commanding figure with a strong jaw, dark complexion, and a regal posture
clothing_notes: Royal white and gold antariya (lower garment) with an uttariya draped over one shoulder, jeweled crown, heavy gold necklaces, armlets, and earrings in Mauryan court style

name: Shivaji
description: Founder of the Maratha Empire in western India, pioneered guerrilla warfare tactics against the Mughal Empire, and established a competent civil administration with emphasis on religious tolerance and Marathi culture
physical_description: Medium build with a strong martial bearing, prominent mustache, alert eyes, and a determined expression
clothing_notes: Maratha-style pagdi (turban) with a jeweled ornament, angarkha (long coat) over a fitted kurta, dhoti, and a curved sword (talwar) at the waist; armor for battle depictions
